REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (164 Maddux Ave., San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Jul-25-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Thomas Lucas.

The sale was confirmed at $398,200.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $398,000.00 is required. Ms. Avid stated that the additional bond was miscalculated and that it should be in the amount of $377,000.00. The Court will address/review the bond issue prior to signing the order.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

Off Calendar Oct-05-2011 Continued To Nov-02-11 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Anderson appeared by telephone for Deborah King. Edward Broitman for Robert Johnson.

The Second Amended First Account was continued to November 2, 2011 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 as well as the Supplemental Accounting which was set for October 31, 2011. The Court ordered Mr. Broitman to prepare an order substituting Thomas Lucas in as Succesor Trustee and removing Deborah King as Trustee. Bond will be set at $10,000.00. Ms. King needs to file a First and Final Account as of the date of the order and needs to cooperate with Mr. Lucas. Status of the order was also set for that date. Mr. Anderson will appear by telephone.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Aguerre.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Ms. King'S Accounting
Off Calendar Jul-19-2011 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Broitman for Robert Johnson (Petitioner). David Anderson appeared by telephone for Deborah King (Trustee).

Mr. Anderson stated that the accounting is completed, that he will be filing it today, that accounting covers from the date of death to February 11, 2011 and that he anticipates filing an account from February 11, 2011 to present shortly. With regard to the real property, Mr. Anderson stated that the Trustee will be signing the listing agreement today, that there may be a problem with removing the Tenant, that they are hopeful of working out a voluntary leaving of the premises by the Tenant and that they agreed on a sales price. Status of the accounting and status of the sale of the real property were taken off calendar. Mr. Anderson will be allowed to appear by courtcall at the next hearing.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Regarding Sale Of The Real Property
Off Calendar Jul-19-2011 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Regarding Sale Of The Real Property
Off Calendar Jun-28-2011 Continued To Jul-19-11 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Anderson for and with Deborah King and Lakesha King. Edward Broitman for and with Robert King.

Mr. Anderson stated that they agreed on a Realtor to sell the property, that the house was appraised at $400,000.00 and that the Realtor will be viewing the house this afternoon. Mr. Broitman raised the issue of the house going down in value, drop in value perhaps having to do with a Tenant living in the house under Section 8 and the possibility of a 90-day notice to the Tenant being required. The matter was continued to July 19, 2011 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel can appear by court call. The Court suggested the Parties consult with a Landlord Tenant Attorney.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Regarding Sale Of The Real Property
Off Calendar Jun-14-2011 Continued To Jun-28-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Anderson for and with Deborah King. Edward Broitman for Robert Johnson. Charles Johnson in Propria Persona.

Counsel reported that Ms. King and the other Family members are not able to do a buy out and that the property needs to be sold. The Parties could not agree as to Mr. Lucas being appointed as Successor Trustee and Ms. King being removed. The Court expressed concern about additional cost being incurred. Mr. Anderson stated that Ms. King was willing to waive all Trustee fees if she remians as Trustee and winds up the Estate/sale of the house. Status of the property and status on a Successor Trustee was continued to June 28, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to see if the Parties could agree on a Realtor and a sales price. Status on Ms. King's accounting was set for July 19, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harrison.

Petition Of Beneficiary To Remove Trustee, To Appoint Successor Truatee And To Direct Current Trustee To Report And Account, To Transfer Real Property To Trust And To Surcharge Trustee And Award Attorney'S Fees And Damages.
Off Calendar May-09-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Broitman for and with Robert Johnson. David Anderson for and with Deborah King. Charles Johnson, Danny Johnson and Lakisha King were also present.

Counsel stated that they particpated in mediation and that they were unable to resolve the matter. The parties were asked to step out of the Courtroom and discuss the matter. Upon returning, Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to how title of the property is held, having an independent person in place, leasing the property, rents, distribution of lease/rental income, taxes/penalties, conflict of interest with other named Trustees, selling the house and requested the Court make a ruling on all matters before the Court. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Off Calendar Feb-16-2011 Continued To Mar-30-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Broitman for and with Robert Johnson. David Anderson for and with Debra King (Trustee). Charles Johnson in Propria Persona. Lakisha King in Propria Persona.

The Parties discussed the matter outside of the courtroom. Upon returning, Mr. Anderson stated that Ms. King will file a petition for instructions regarding interpretation of certain terms in the Trust. Mr. Broitman stated that he has an acceptance of appointment from Thomas Lucas. The Court informed Mr. Broitman that a supplement to the petition needs to be filed. The matter was continued to March 30, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation (order signed in open Court). Mediation is to be completed by March 30, 2011. The Parties are to report to the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by March 25, 2011.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Ms. King stated that she was out of town due to a death in the family and that she was in the process of retaining a Lawyer. MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Broitman for Robert Johnson.

Mr. Broitman stated that he did not receive the accounting from Ms. King. The Court will issue an Order to Appear to Ms. King to appear on January 12, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Today's matter was also continued to January 12, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account.
Off Calendar Nov-15-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Broitman for and with Robert Johnson (Petitioner).

The Court informed Counsel and Mr. Johnson that Ms. King had provided a copy of the Trust to the Probate Examiner yesterday. The Court provided a copy to Counsel and stated that it was the Court's understanding that the property goes to the four Children. Mr. Broitman stated that Ms. King deeded the property into her personal name, that he obtained a copy of Quit Claim Deed, raised the issue of Ms. King not following the instructions of the Trust and not performing her duties, and requested an accounting. The Court granted the Petition to Compel Trustee to Account (subject to review/modification of the order). Status of filing of Ms. King's first account was set for December 14, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-09-2010 Text Ruling

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account.
Off Calendar Oct-19-2010 Continued To Nov-09-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Johnson in Propria Persona. Debra King in Propria Persona. Lakeshia King also present.

Debra King stated that she could not find a copy of the Trust, that she received a letter from the company that prepared the Trust stating that they were in bankruptcy and that they were not able to assist her (letter provided), that she was attempting to contact the Notary Public and acknowledged that Mr. Johnson was a beneficiary of the Trust. Debra King also expressed her Mother's wishes and stated that the house was being rented out. Mr. Johnson expressed his concerns and frustration. The matter was continued to November 9, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Debra King is ordered to appear on that date. The Court also directed Debra King to contact her Brothers and try to obtain a copy of the Trust.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Reporter: Robert Balian, CSR#5220. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-19-2010 Continued To Nov-09-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account.
Off Calendar Sep-21-2010 Continued To Oct-19-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Johnson in Propria Persona.

Debra King called the Clerk and stated that she was unable to appear as she was ill. Mr. Johnson stated that he had not received a copy of the Trust, that he sent notification of his change of address by certified mail to the Court as well as to Ms. King and expressed frustration with regard to the matter being continued numerous times and nothing being done. The matter was continued to October 19, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. An Order to Show Cause will be issued to Ms. King to appear and explain why she has not complied with the previous direction/order of the Court to provide a copy of the Trust to Robert Johnson and to the Court. The return date is October 19, at 9:00 a.m. , in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account.
Off Calendar Jul-27-2010 Continued To Sep-21-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Johnson appeared by telephone in Propria Persona. Deborah King in Propria Persona. Lakisha King in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that the law provides that any Beneficiaries or Heirs are entitled to receive a copy of the Trust. The Parties presented oral argument as to whether Mr. Johnson did or did not receive a copy of the Trust, spoke of the Family dynamics and the difficulty in communication. The Court directed Deborah King to send a copy of the Trust to the address provided in Court by Mr. Johnson and to file a proof of service. The Court also stated that it also needed to see a copy of the Trust so that it can determine if Robert Johnson has standing. The Petition to Compel Trustee to Account was continued to September 21, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Order to Show Cause was discharged.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-27-2010 Text Ruling

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-27-2010 Text Ruling

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account.
Off Calendar Jun-01-2010 Continued To Jul-27-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Johnson in Propria Persona.

Mr. Johnson stated that he had not received a copy of the Trust from Ms. King and that he had no contact with her at all. The Court noted that the Order to Show Cause issued on May 12, 2010 was for Deborah King to appear and explain why she has not complied with the Court's prior order and turn over a copy of the Trust. The Court encouraged Mr. Johnson to seek the assistance of an Attorney. A further Order to Show Cause will be issued to the Trustee to appear and show why sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day should not be imposed for failure to appear, for failure to provide a copy of the Trust and for failure to provide notice to the Beneficiaries. The return date is July 27, 2010 at 9:00 a.m, in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-01-2010 Continued To Jul-27-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account.
Off Calendar May-04-2010 Continued To Jun-01-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Johnson in Propria Persona.

Mr. Johnson stated that he has not received a copy of the Trust from the Trustee and that he has had no contact with her. An Order to Show Cause will be issued to the Trustee to appear and explain why she has not comlpied with the Court's prior order to turn over a copy of the Trust and provide notice to the Beneficiaries. The return date is June 1, 2010 at 9:00 a.m, in Dept. 204. The Petition to Compel Trustee to Account was also contiuned to June 1, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account.
Off Calendar Mar-30-2010 Continued To May-04-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Johnson in pro per.

Mr. Johnson stated that he did not get a copy of the Trust. The Court stated that it would send an order to the Trustee (Debora King) ordering her to give notice to all the heirs and provide all of them with a copy of the Trust. The Court informed Mr. Johnson that his first step is to get a copy of the Trust. The Court contined the petition to compel to May 4, 2010, and ordered that the Trustee turn over the copies by that same date.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Court Reporter: Nici Rodich, CSR #11604. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
